sections.paragraph:
πλημμέλεια, ας, ἡ (πλημμελέω ‘err’; Democr. 46; Pla. et al.; LXX) the prim. sense, ‘false note’ in music, leads to the fig. ext.: someth. that is contrary to a generally recognized standard, freq. of cultic or legal violation: fault, error, sin, offense (Aristot. 1251a, 31 ἀσέβεια ἡ περὶ θεοὺς πλ.; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 250; restored in POxy 850 [AcJ], 30) θυσίαι περὶ ἁμαρτίας καὶ πλημμελείας sin and offense (trespass) offerings (LXX, esp. Lev 7:37) 1 Cl 41:2. Pl. (Plut., Mor. 168d ἁμαρτίαι κ. πλημμέλειαι; Philo, Mos. 2, 230; Jos., Bell. 5, 392) 60:1 (w. ἀνομίαι, ἀδικίαι, παραπτώματα).—Dodd 76.—DELG s.v. πλημμελής.

Democr Democr , phil., V/IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

prim. prim. = primarily

ext. ext. = extension

someth. someth. = something

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

AcJ AcJ = Acts of John—List 5

esp. esp. = especially

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Dodd Dodd = CD., The Bible and the Greeks—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 826
